meta.label: Seismic Acquisition
meta.description: Classes, properties, relations and instances covering seismic survey acquisition: survey types, sources, receivers, geometry and sampling parameters.
sources: Yilmaz, O. (2001). Seismic Data Analysis. SEG Investigations in Geophysics No. 10.
Sheriff, R.E. & Geldart, L.P. (1995). Exploration Seismology (2nd ed.). Cambridge University Press.

SeismicSurvey.name: SeismicSurvey
SeismicSurvey.description: A controlled experiment in which seismic energy is injected into the Earth and the returning wavefield is recorded to image subsurface structure. Encompasses geometry definition, source specification and receiver deployment. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1.

LandSurvey.name: LandSurvey
LandSurvey.description: Onshore seismic survey in which sources (vibroseis, dynamite) and receivers (geophones) are deployed on land. Near-surface weathering layer and statics corrections are dominant challenges. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 3.

MarineSurvey.name: MarineSurvey
MarineSurvey.description: Offshore towed-streamer survey in which a vessel tows one or more hydrophone cables. Dominant configuration for deep-water exploration. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1; Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

OBNSurvey.name: OBNSurvey
OBNSurvey.description: Ocean-Bottom Node survey: autonomous 4C (Vx, Vy, Vz + hydrophone) sensor packages placed on the seabed. Enables full-azimuth illumination and longer offsets than towed streamers. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

OBSSurvey.name: OBSSurvey
OBSSurvey.description: Ocean-Bottom Seismometer/Cable survey: 4C sensors deployed on cables laid on the sea floor, offering P- and S-wave recording for joint PP/PS inversion.

Source.name: Source
Source.description: Device or process that generates seismic energy and injects it into the ground or water. Characterised by peak frequency, dominant frequency band and energy output. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 2.

Airgun.name: Airgun
Airgun.description: Marine seismic source that releases compressed air (typically 2000 psi) into the water to generate an impulsive pressure wavelet. Standard deep-water acquisition source. Peak frequency range 10-100 Hz. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

Vibroseis.name: Vibroseis
Vibroseis.description: Land seismic source that emits a swept-frequency (chirp) signal via a vibrating baseplate hydraulically coupled to the ground. Allows controlled bandwidth 6-120 Hz. Requires cross-correlation with pilot sweep to produce zero-phase wavelet. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 2.

Dynamite.name: Dynamite
Dynamite.description: Explosive seismic source detonated in a shallow drill hole to generate a broad-band (5-200 Hz) near-impulsive wavelet. Preferred in areas of complex near-surface topography where vibroseis coupling is poor. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

SparkerArray.name: SparkerArray
SparkerArray.description: High-resolution marine source using electrical discharge to vaporise water and generate a broadband (50-1000 Hz) pressure pulse. Used in ultra-high-resolution shallow surveys. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

Receiver.name: Receiver
Receiver.description: Sensor that converts mechanical ground or water motion into an electrical signal. Sensitivity, natural frequency and dynamic range determine the recordable bandwidth. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 2.

Hydrophone.name: Hydrophone
Hydrophone.description: Pressure-sensitive piezoelectric sensor towed in marine streamers. Records scalar pressure wavefield (P-wave only). Sensitivity ~10 mV/Pa. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

Geophone.name: Geophone
Geophone.description: Velocity-sensitive electromagnetic sensor planted on the ground surface. Natural frequency typically 10 Hz (land) or 4.5 Hz (OBC). Records particle velocity. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 2.

AccelerometerMEMS.name: AccelerometerMEMS
AccelerometerMEMS.description: Micro-Electro-Mechanical System accelerometer used in nodal acquisition systems. Flat response from DC to several hundred Hz; measures particle acceleration. Superior low-frequency response compared to geophone. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

OBC.name: OBC
OBC.description: Ocean-Bottom Cable: multi-component cable laid on the sea floor carrying collocated hydrophones and geophones (4C). Enables dual-sensor summation and S-wave recording. Ref: Sheriff & Geldart 1995.

AcquisitionGeometry.name: AcquisitionGeometry
AcquisitionGeometry.description: Spatial arrangement of sources and receivers defining illumination, fold, offset distribution and azimuth coverage. Critical for migration operator accuracy. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1.

StreamerLayout.name: StreamerLayout
StreamerLayout.description: Marine geometry in which one or more hydrophone cables (streamers) are towed behind a vessel. Characterised by streamer length, cable separation, tow depth and number of streamers.

NodalLayout.name: NodalLayout
NodalLayout.description: Land or OBN geometry employing autonomous wireless nodes deployed in a regular or irregular grid. Enables full-azimuth illumination.

SamplingParameters.name: SamplingParameters
SamplingParameters.description: Temporal and spatial sampling parameters that define the recorded wavefield fidelity and alias constraints. Must satisfy the Nyquist theorem in both time and space. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1.

Aliasing.name: Aliasing
Aliasing.description: Artifact arising when the sampling interval violates the Nyquist criterion, causing high-frequency energy to fold back into the recorded spectrum. Temporal aliasing: dt > 1/(2*fmax). Spatial aliasing: dx > v/(2*fmax). Ref: Yilmaz 2001, ch. 1.

samplingInterval.name: samplingInterval
samplingInterval.description: Time interval between consecutive recorded samples. Standard values: 1 ms (high-resolution), 2 ms (conventional), 4 ms (low-frequency). Must be dt <= 1/(2*fNyquist). Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

recordLength.name: recordLength
recordLength.description: Total duration of each recorded seismic trace, from shot instant to end of recording. Typical 6-10 s for deep targets.

fold.name: fold
fold.description: Number of independent seismic traces that contribute to a single stacked trace after CMP sorting. Higher fold improves signal-to-noise ratio. Standard 3D: 60-120x. Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

offset.name: offset
offset.description: Distance between source and receiver. Minimum offset determines shallow resolution; maximum offset determines depth penetration and NMO velocity resolution. Must be non-negative. Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

NyquistFrequency.name: NyquistFrequency
NyquistFrequency.description: Maximum alias-free frequency: fN = 1/(2*dt). For dt=2ms, fN=250Hz. Ref: Yilmaz 2001, eq. 1-1.

signalBandwidth.name: signalBandwidth
signalBandwidth.description: Frequency range of meaningful signal energy after instrument and earth filtering. Typical 8-80 Hz for conventional reflection seismics.

peakFrequency.name: peakFrequency
peakFrequency.description: Dominant frequency of the source wavelet or Ricker wavelet. Determines vertical resolution: lambda/4 = v/(4*fPeak). Ref: Yilmaz 2001.

operatingPressure.name: operatingPressure
operatingPressure.description: Working pressure of an airgun array at fire time. Typical value 138 bar (2000 psi). Controls peak-to-bubble ratio.

sweepFrequencyMin.name: sweepFrequencyMin
sweepFrequencyMin.description: Lowest frequency in the vibroseis pilot sweep.

sweepFrequencyMax.name: sweepFrequencyMax
sweepFrequencyMax.description: Highest frequency in the vibroseis pilot sweep.

sweepLength.name: sweepLength
sweepLength.description: Duration of the vibroseis sweep signal in seconds. Longer sweeps improve signal-to-noise ratio after correlation.

streamerLength.name: streamerLength
streamerLength.description: Total active length of a towed hydrophone cable. Controls maximum offset and hence depth penetration.

numberOfStreamers.name: numberOfStreamers
numberOfStreamers.description: Count of towed hydrophone cables. Increases cross-line coverage and survey efficiency.

gridSpacing.name: gridSpacing
gridSpacing.description: In-line and cross-line CMP bin dimensions. Determines lateral spatial aliasing limit: dx <= v/(2*fmax). Typical 12.5x12.5m to 25x25m in 3D surveys.

waterDepth.name: waterDepth
waterDepth.description: Water depth at survey location. Determines ghost frequency, node deployment logistics and cable operation window.

receiverSensitivity.name: receiverSensitivity
receiverSensitivity.description: Transduction efficiency of a receiver. For hydrophones: mV/Pa; for geophones: V/(m/s).

surveyArea.name: surveyArea
surveyArea.description: Total area covered by the seismic survey polygon.

uses_source.name: uses_source
uses_source.description: A SeismicSurvey uses one or more Source devices to generate seismic energy.

deploys_receiver.name: deploys_receiver
deploys_receiver.description: A SeismicSurvey deploys one or more Receiver types.

applies_geometry.name: applies_geometry
applies_geometry.description: A SeismicSurvey is characterised by an AcquisitionGeometry.

respects_nyquist.name: respects_nyquist
respects_nyquist.description: SamplingParameters respects the Nyquist criterion with respect to the signal bandwidth: samplingInterval <= 1/(2*signalBandwidthMax). Violation produces Aliasing.

characterised_by.name: characterised_by
characterised_by.description: A SeismicSurvey is characterised by SamplingParameters.

produces_aliasing.name: produces_aliasing
produces_aliasing.description: Violation of Nyquist constraint produces Aliasing artifact.

is_part_of_survey.name: is_part_of_survey
is_part_of_survey.description: AcquisitionGeometry or SamplingParameters is defined as part of a SeismicSurvey.

collocated_with.name: collocated_with
collocated_with.description: In OBC/OBN deployments a Hydrophone is collocated with a Geophone or AccelerometerMEMS at the same spatial position.

I_BUZ3D_2022.name: Buzios 3D Wide-Azimuth Streamer Survey 2022
I_BUZ3D_2022.description: Broadband wide-azimuth 3D towed-streamer survey over the Buzios carbonate pre-salt field, Santos Basin, offshore Brazil. 14 streamers x 8 km, 100 m cable separation, 6.25 m group interval, 2 ms sample interval.
I_BUZ3D_2022.source: Analogous to Buzios-field 3D acquisition programs described in Petrobras technical literature

I_LAND_VIB_2021.name: Parnaiba Basin Vibroseis Land Survey 2021
I_LAND_VIB_2021.name_pt: Levantamento Terrestre Vibrosseis Bacia do Parnaiba 2021
I_LAND_VIB_2021.description: Orthogonal-template 3D vibroseis survey in the Parnaiba Basin, northeastern Brazil. Four vibrator fleet, 4-8 Hz linear sweep, 16 s sweep length, 2 ms sample interval, maximum offset 6000 m.
I_LAND_VIB_2021.source: Representative of ANP-regulated onshore 3D programs in Brazilian interior basins

I_OBN_LULA_2023.name: Lula OBN 4C Full-Azimuth Survey 2023
I_OBN_LULA_2023.description: Ocean-Bottom Node full-azimuth 4C survey over the Lula giant pre-salt field, Santos Basin. Nodes deployed at 2100 m water depth on 400 x 400 m grid; P- and S-wave data recorded for joint PP/PS inversion and imaging through the salt canopy.
I_OBN_LULA_2023.source: Analogous to Petrobras OBN programs in Santos Basin ultra-deep water
